skill.name: shadcn-ui
skill.description: Guide for building React and Next.js interfaces with shadcn/ui, Radix UI, Tailwind CSS, React Hook Form, and Zod.
skill.summary: Build accessible shadcn/ui components, forms, themes, and layouts in React projects.
skill.author: giuseppe-trisciuoglio

security_audit.summary: The static critical heuristic is dismissed because the matched content is markdown documentation and examples, not hidden executable code. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it guides agents to run package-manager commands, fetch remote resources, edit project files, and handle registry tokens.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Commands Can Modify Projects
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs agents to run npm, npx, pnpm, and shadcn CLI commands. These commands are legitimate for shadcn/ui setup, but they fetch remote packages and modify local projects.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and frequent, and they can change a project or install remote dependencies. Context shows normal developer setup guidance, not malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Registry Examples Target Hidden User Configuration Files
medium_findings.description: Reference examples show registry items writing to hidden files in a user home directory, including ESLint and Cursor configuration paths. This is not automatically executed, but agents following the examples could overwrite user configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The target paths are visible in markdown examples and can affect hidden configuration files. The risk is limited because the files contain reference snippets, not active installer code.

low_findings.title: Environment Token Handling Appears in Examples
low_findings.description: The references describe registry tokens, .env files, and a placeholder process.env token check. No evidence shows token exfiltration, but copied examples should protect secrets carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is real but appears in documentation for private registries and placeholder authentication. I did not find network transmission of environment secrets.

low_findings.title: Network Patterns Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Hardcoded documentation URLs and local fetch examples triggered scanner network findings. They are normal references and application examples, not hidden outbound calls by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network strings are visible educational examples and official resource links. There is no executable wrapper that sends local data to those endpoints.

low_findings.title: Static Code-Execution Heuristic Overmatched Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The many Ruby backtick and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from markdown fences, import aliases, dates, colors, and sample data. No executable Ruby or cryptographic routine was found in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are fenced examples or prose. The files are markdown guides, and I did not find hidden scripts or prompt-injection text.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent React interfaces without rebuilding common components from scratch. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through shadcn/ui setup, forms, theming, and component patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains shadcn/ui installation for new and existing React projects.
Provides component patterns for buttons, dialogs, cards, tables, sheets, and selects.
Shows form construction with React Hook Form and Zod validation.
Documents Tailwind CSS configuration and theme customization patterns.
References registry, private package, and component copy workflows.
Includes learning guidance and practice exercises for shadcn/ui adoption.
limitations: It is a documentation skill, not an automated component generator.
Some referenced package versions may need verification before installation.
Examples may require adaptation for project structure and framework version.
Registry and authentication examples need careful secret handling.

use_cases.title: Start a shadcn/ui Project
use_cases.description: Set up shadcn/ui in a Next.js or React project with Tailwind CSS and core components.

use_cases.title: Build Validated Forms
use_cases.description: Create accessible forms using shadcn/ui fields, React Hook Form, and Zod schemas.

use_cases.title: Standardize UI Patterns
use_cases.description: Document reusable component patterns, variants, themes, and layout conventions for a product team.

prompt_templates.title: Install Core Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up shadcn/ui in this React project. Check the framework first, then recommend the safest install commands.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a shadcn/ui contact form with React Hook Form and Zod. Include validation states, loading feedback, and accessible labels.

prompt_templates.title: Customize a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this shadcn/ui component and add variants that match our Tailwind theme. Keep accessibility and type safety intact.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Registry Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a shadcn/ui registry workflow for shared internal components. Include file targets, authentication risks, and review steps.

output_examples.input: Set up shadcn/ui in an existing Next.js app.
output_examples.output: A project-specific setup plan with prerequisite checks, install commands, component choices, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: Create a settings page with cards, switches, selects, and toast messages.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation outline covering component selection, state handling, validation, and accessible feedback.
A concise review of Tailwind tokens, CSS variables, component consistency, and changes needed before rollout.

best_practices: Confirm the project framework and package manager before running install commands.
Review generated component files before committing them to the repository.
Keep tokens and registry credentials outside shared examples and source control.
anti_patterns: Running every referenced command without checking the local project state.
Copying registry examples that write to home directory files without approval.
Treating placeholder authentication examples as production-ready security logic.

faq.question: Does this skill install shadcn/ui automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and command examples that an agent or developer can choose to run.

faq.question: Can it help with existing React projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes setup guidance for existing projects and explains required dependencies.

faq.question: Does it cover forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents React Hook Form, Zod schemas, field components, validation, and submit handling.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The content is legitimate, but it includes package commands, registry examples, and secret-handling guidance.

faq.question: Are the scanner critical findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. Manual review found markdown examples and prose, not hidden malicious execution.

faq.question: What should users review before applying examples?
faq.answer: Users should review install commands, generated files, registry targets, and any environment variable usage.
